en.extract:
Tristram's bunting (Emberiza tristrami) is a bird in the family Emberizidae. The species was first described by Robert Swinhoe in 1870.
It breeds in eastern Manchuria and the Russian Far East and winters in central and southern China.
It is accidental in Japan, Korea, Laos, Myanmar, Russia, Mongolia, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam and north-eastern India. Its natural habitat is boreal forests.

'''Tristram's bunting''' ('''''Emberiza tristrami''''') is a [[bird]] in the family [[Emberizidae]]. The species was [[Species description|first described]] by [[Robert Swinhoe]] in 1870.

It breeds in eastern [[Manchuria]] and the [[Russian Far East]] and winters in central and southern [[China]].

It is accidental in Japan, Korea, Laos, Myanmar, Russia, Mongolia, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam and north-eastern India.<ref>{{cite journal|last=Naniwadekar|first=Rohit|author2=Ashwin Viswanathan |author3=Raman Kumar |author4=Shashank Dalvi |title=First record of Tristram's Bunting ''Emberiza tristrami'' from India|journal=Indian Birds|date=5 September 2013|volume=8|issue=5|page=134|url=https://www.academia.edu/4791781 |access-date=20 September 2015}}</ref> Its natural [[habitat]] is [[boreal forest]]s.

zh.extract:
白眉鹀（学名：Emberiza tristrami）为鵐科鹀属的鸟类，俗名白三道儿、小白眉、五道眉。分布于俄罗斯、朝鲜半岛、缅甸以及中国大陆的东北、内蒙古、河北、河南、湖北、山东、江苏、浙江、福建、广东、广西、四川、云南等地，常见于山地森林、栖息于针阔混交林和针叶林带以及喜在山溪沟谷、林缘、林间空地和林下灌丛或草丛活动。该物种的模式产地在福建厦门。
